questions.question: The diagram shows the distance-time graph of an object. 0 0 distance time Which statement describes the object? A It is accelerating., B It is moving at a constant speed., C It is slowing down., D It is stationary. 3
questions.text: The diagram shows the distance-time graph of an object. 0 0 distance time Which statement describes the object? A It is accelerating., B It is moving at a constant speed., C It is slowing down., D It is stationary. 3
options: B It is moving at a constant speed.

questions.question: A tennis player hits a ball hard and 0.40 s later hears the echo from a wall. The speed of sound in air is 330 m / s. How far away is the player from the wall? A 66 m B 132 m C 264 m D 825 m
questions.text: A tennis player hits a ball hard and 0.40 s later hears the echo from a wall. The speed of sound in air is 330 m / s. How far away is the player from the wall? A 66 m B 132 m C 264 m D 825 m

questions.question: A car travels along a horizontal road at a constant speed. Three horizontal forces act on the car. The diagram shows two of these three forces. air resistance 300 N force from engine 1500 N direction of motion forwards What is the size and the direction of the third horizontal force acting on the car? A 1200 N backwards, B 1200 N forwards, C 1800 N backwards, D 1800 N forwards

questions.question: Four different model steam engines each lift a 1.0 kg object from the same laboratory floor to the same laboratory bench. Each engine takes a different time to lift the object. How does the most powerful engine compare with the other engines? speed of lifting object onto bench useful work done A faster more than other engines, B faster same as other engines, C slower less than other engines, D slower same as other engines
questions.text: Four different model steam engines each lift a 1.0 kg object from the same laboratory floor to the same laboratory bench. Each engine takes a different time to lift the object. How does the most powerful engine compare with the other engines? speed of lifting object onto bench useful work done A faster more than other engines, B faster same as other engines, C slower less than other engines, D slower same as other engines
options: A faster more than other engines
B faster same as other engines
C slower less than other engines
D slower same as other engines

questions.question: Which statement is explained by reference to pressure? A Objects with greater mass have greater weight., B One kilogram of water occupies more volume than one kilogram of lead., C Spikes on running-shoes sink into the ground., D Water cooled to a low enough temperature turns to ice.
questions.text: Which statement is explained by reference to pressure? A Objects with greater mass have greater weight., B One kilogram of water occupies more volume than one kilogram of lead., C Spikes on running-shoes sink into the ground., D Water cooled to a low enough temperature turns to ice.
options: A Objects with greater mass have greater weight.
B One kilogram of water occupies more volume than one kilogram of lead.
C Spikes on running-shoes sink into the ground.
D Water cooled to a low enough temperature turns to ice.

questions.question: Small smoke particles suspended in air are viewed through a microscope. The smoke particles move randomly. What does this show? A The air consists of fast-moving molecules., B The pressure of the air is increasing., C There are convection currents in the air., D The temperature of the air is increasing. 6
questions.text: Small smoke particles suspended in air are viewed through a microscope. The smoke particles move randomly. What does this show? A The air consists of fast-moving molecules., B The pressure of the air is increasing., C There are convection currents in the air., D The temperature of the air is increasing. 6
options: A The air consists of fast-moving molecules.
B The pressure of the air is increasing.
C There are convection currents in the air.
D The temperature of the air is increasing. 6

questions.question: The gas in a container is heated but is kept at constant volume. Why does the gas pressure increase? A The   molecules   expand., B The molecules increase in mass., C The molecules move further apart., D The molecules move more rapidly.
questions.text: The gas in a container is heated but is kept at constant volume. Why does the gas pressure increase? A The   molecules   expand., B The molecules increase in mass., C The molecules move further apart., D The molecules move more rapidly.
options: B The molecules increase in mass.
C The molecules move further apart.
D The molecules move more rapidly.

questions.question: The thermal capacity of solid Y is greater than that of solid Z. What is a consequence of this? A Solid Y needs less thermal energy to melt it than solid Z., B Solid Y needs less thermal energy to raise its temperature by 1 °C than solid Z., C Solid Y needs more thermal energy to melt it than solid Z., D Solid Y needs more thermal energy to raise its temperature by 1 °C than solid Z.
questions.text: The thermal capacity of solid Y is greater than that of solid Z. What is a consequence of this? A Solid Y needs less thermal energy to melt it than solid Z., B Solid Y needs less thermal energy to raise its temperature by 1 °C than solid Z., C Solid Y needs more thermal energy to melt it than solid Z., D Solid Y needs more thermal energy to raise its temperature by 1 °C than solid Z.
options: A Solid Y needs less thermal energy to melt it than solid Z.
B Solid Y needs less thermal energy to raise its temperature by 1 °C than solid Z.
C Solid Y needs more thermal energy to melt it than solid Z.
D Solid Y needs more thermal energy to raise its temperature by 1 °C than solid Z.

questions.question: In the experiment shown, the bell is heard ringing. The air is gradually pumped out of the jar. No change is made to the ringing bell. air pump rubber bands bell glass jar After a few minutes the bell can no longer be heard. Why is this? A The amplitude of vibration of the bell decreases., B The frequency of vibration of the bell increases., C The sound waves from the bell become transverse., D The sound waves need a medium to travel through.
questions.text: In the experiment shown, the bell is heard ringing. The air is gradually pumped out of the jar. options: A The amplitude of vibration of the bell decreases.
B The frequency of vibration of the bell increases.
C The sound waves from the bell become transverse.
D The sound waves need a medium to travel through.

P coil core iron nails QR + – + – + – The three diagrams below show what happens when the current is then switched off.
